name: 14-story hotel building
description: A large facility contributing to high waste volume at the Red Building area.

name: Adding more trash in the morning
description: The act of depositing new waste into already full bins/cages at the start of the day.

name: Ants
description: Pests attracted to organic food waste left in office spaces.

name: BMA
description: The Bangkok Metropolitan Administration; the final destination for non-recyclable waste disposal.

name: Building PM
description: Property Manager responsible for contacting coordinators when waste needs pickup.

name: Changing the policy
description: A simulated adjustment in waste management rules to test system improvements.

name: Chemical spills
description: Hazardous incidents resulting from the failure to follow laboratory waste regulations.

name: Cleaning waste
description: The process of washing general waste to make it suitable for fuel (RDF).

name: Committee/President
description: University leadership who respond to and issue warnings regarding waste overflow.

name: Common trash (General waste)
description: Daily waste that is either cleaned for fuel or sent to BMA for disposal.

name: Complicated weighing process
description: The current manual task of weighing trash which leads to worker avoidance and data gaps.

name: Construction waste
description: Includes materials like iron; cement; wood; and plywood from renovations or events.

name: Convenience
description: A psychological factor where individuals dump waste in the nearest bin regardless of its type.

name: COVID-19 situation
description: A specific period that necessitated the introduction of infectious waste bins for ATK kits and masks.

name: Department/Faculty
description: Organizational units within the university that generate waste and receive revenue shares.

name: Donating e-waste
description: The act of giving electronics to the sorting center in exchange for activity hours.

name: External landfill
description: The final disposal site for unusable construction waste located near Wat Bua Phan.

name: Finance Division
description: The department that processes waste data to distribute payments to university units.

name: Garden team
description: The crew responsible for collecting external university waste using trucks.

name: Gray bins
description: Specific containers designated for construction materials and debris.

name: High volume of recyclable waste
description: A condition that triggers the need for special pickup schedules outside regular dates.

name: Hiring additional staff
description: A potential solution for better management that results in increased financial costs.

name: Housekeepers (Maids)
description: Primary personnel who collect; weigh; and move waste from buildings to collection points.

name: Inaccurate data/Missing data
description: The result of human error; guessing numbers; or skipping the weighing process entirely.

name: Internal land/External waste
description: Distinction between waste generated inside buildings versus on campus grounds.

name: Lab regulations
description: Rules governing the handling of hazardous materials to prevent injury or spills.

name: Late night activities
description: Events running from midnight to 2 AM that result in sudden waste accumulation.

name: Limited budget
description: The financial constraint that led to the current (often problematic) waste cage design.

name: Maids' mood
description: A subjective factor that influences whether data is recorded accurately or fabricated.

name: Mirror Foundation
description: A recipient organization that uses reusable e-waste parts to repair computers for rural children.

name: N15
description: An external organization that accepts cleaned waste to be used as Refuse-Derived Fuel (RDF).

name: Optimizing the system
description: The goal of improving waste management efficiency through simulation or better design.

name: Pickup rounds
description: Scheduled times (standard or requested) for transporting waste from buildings to sorting plants.

name: Piling waste in front of buildings
description: A practice for monthly recycling rounds where waste is left for early morning pickup.

name: Rats
description: Pests attracted to unmanaged food waste in buildings.

name: Renting reusable equipment
description: An alternative to single-use items (plates/cups) that significantly reduces waste weight.

name: Revenue from unit waste
description: Funds generated from recycling; split 80% to the unit and 20% to the university.

name: Shop space
description: Designated areas in certain faculties where waste is stored internally before direct collection.

name: Sorting facility/Plant
description: The central hub where waste is aggregated; processed; and prepared for final disposal or reuse.

name: Staff/Personnel
description: University employees who generate waste and are responsible for following disposal rules.

name: Students
description: The primary generators of waste; often cited as a factor in uncontrolled waste piles and improper sorting.

name: Trash cans/Bins
description: The physical equipment used for disposal; noted for lacking integrated weighing mechanisms.

name: Trash overflow
description: A persistent problem where waste exceeds the capacity of bins or cages; especially in the mornings.

name: Tree debris
description: Organic waste that is processed into fertilizer.

name: Units and offices
description: Individual university divisions that label and bring down waste to designated spots.

name: Waste cage
description: The structure used to hold waste; currently described as too small and needing a roof.

name: Waste data
description: Information regarding the weight and type of trash used for financial and management tracking.

name: Waste residue
description: Trash that remains overnight because it was deposited after the final collection round.

name: Yellow BMA bins
description: Compression bins used for unsortable waste that is sent for city-level disposal.
